schema_context:
Table: Best Artworks of All Time
Rows: 50
Columns: 8

FIELD INFORMATION:
Field: id
  - Data Type: int64
  - Inferred Type: number
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents a unique identifier assigned to each artwork in the dataset, enabling easy reference and distinction among the 50 listed "Best Artworks of All Time."
  - Value Range: 0.0 to 49.0
  - Mean: 24.50

Field: name
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents the names of renowned artists whose works are featured in the "Best Artworks of All Time" dataset, identifying the creator of each artwork.

Field: years
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents the time span (years) during which each listed artwork was created or completed, indicating the period of artistic production for masterpieces in the art history domain.

Field: genre
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 31
  - Semantic Meaning: The "genre" field categorizes each artwork by its artistic style or movement, often listing one or more relevant genres to describe the artwork's aesthetic or historical context.

Field: nationality
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 17
  - Semantic Meaning: This field represents the country or countries associated with an artist's origin or cultural background, indicating the national identity relevant to artworks featured in the "Best Artworks of All Time" dataset.

Field: bio
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field contains biographical summaries of notable artists, including their names, birth and death dates, nationalities, and pronunciation or transliterations, providing contextual background for their artworks in the art history domain.

Field: wikipedia
  - Data Type: object
  - Inferred Type: string
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 50
  - Semantic Meaning: This field contains Wikipedia URLs for artists, providing reference links to biographical information about each artist featured among the best artworks of all time.

Field: paintings
  - Data Type: int64
  - Inferred Type: number
  - Null Count: 0
  - Unique Values: 48
  - Semantic Meaning: This field likely represents the total number of paintings created or attributed to each artist in the "Best Artworks of All Time" dataset.
  - Value Range: 24.0 to 877.0
  - Mean: 168.92

DATAFRAME COLUMN NAMES:
The DataFrame will have these EXACT column names that you MUST use for indexing:
  1. "id"
  2. "name"
  3. "years"
  4. "genre"
  5. "nationality"
  6. "bio"
  7. "wikipedia"
  8. "paintings"

CRITICAL: Use ONLY these exact column names when accessing DataFrame columns:
Examples: df['id'], df['name']

  - years -- type: object, values: 1266 - 1337, 1360 - 1430, 1395 - 1441, 1445 - 1510, 1450 - 1516, ..., 1898 - 1967, 1904 - 1989, 1907 - 1954, 1912 – 1956, 1928 – 1987
  - genre -- type: object, values: Abstract Expressionism, Baroque, Byzantine Art, Cubism, Early Renaissance, ..., "Surrealism,Impressionism", Symbolism, "Symbolism,Art Nouveau", "Symbolism,Expressionism", "Symbolism,Post-Impressionism"

He is..., Tiziano Vecelli or Tiziano Vecellio (pronounced [titˈtsjaːno veˈtʃɛlljo]; c. 1488/1490 – 27 August 1..., Vincent Willem van Gogh (Dutch: [ˈvɪnsɛnt ˈʋɪləm vɑŋ ˈɣɔx] (listen); 30 March 1853 – 29 July 1890) w..., "Wassily Wassilyevich Kandinsky (Russian: Васи́лий Васи́льевич Канди́нский, tr. Vasíliy Vasílʹevich K...", Édouard Manet (US: ; UK: ; French: [edwaʁ manɛ]; 23 January 1832 – 30 April 1883) was a French moder...

  - paintings -- type: int64, values: 24, 31, 43, 47, 49, ..., 328, 336, 439, 702, 877

```

0   0   Amedeo Modigliani  1884 - 1920                 Expressionism     Italian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Amedeo Clemente Modigliani (Italian pronunciation: [ameˈdɛːo modiʎˈʎaːni]; 12 July 1884 – 24 January 1920) was an Italian Jewish painter and sculptor who worked mainly in France. He is known for portraits and nudes in a modern style characterized by elongation of faces, necks, and figures that were not received well during his lifetime but later found acceptance. Modigliani spent his youth in Italy, where he studied the art of antiquity and the Renaissance. In 1906 he moved to Paris, where he came into contact with such artists as Pablo Picasso and Constantin Brâncuși. By 1912 Modigliani was exhibiting highly stylized sculptures with Cubists of the Section d'Or group at the Salon d'Automne.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amedeo_Modigliani        193
1   1  Vasiliy Kandinskiy  1866 - 1944  Expressionism,Abstractionism     Russian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Wassily Wassilyevich Kandinsky (Russian: Васи́лий Васи́льевич Канди́нский, tr. Vasíliy Vasílʹevich Kandínskiy) (16 December [O.S. 4 December] 1866 – 13 December 1944) was a Russian painter and art theorist.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wassily_Kandinsky         88
2   2        Diego Rivera  1886 - 1957       Social Realism,Muralism     Mexican                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Diego María de la Concepción Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodríguez, known as Diego Rivera (Spanish pronunciation: [ˈdjeɣo riˈβeɾa]; December 8, 1886 – November 24, 1957) was a prominent Mexican painter. His large frescoes helped establish the Mexican mural movement in Mexican art. Between 1922 and 1953, Rivera painted murals in, among other places, Mexico City, Chapingo, Cuernavaca, San Francisco, Detroit, and New York City. In 1931, a retrospective exhibition of his works was held at the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Rivera had a volatile marriage with fellow Mexican artist Frida Kahlo.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Rivera         70
3   3        Claude Monet  1840 - 1926                 Impressionism      French  Oscar-Claude Monet (; French: [klod mɔnɛ]; 14 November 1840 – 5 December 1926) was a French painter, a founder of French Impressionist painting and the most consistent and prolific practitioner of the movement's philosophy of expressing one's perceptions before nature, especially as applied to plein air landscape painting. The term "Impressionism" is derived from the title of his painting Impression, soleil levant (Impression, Sunrise), which was exhibited in 1874 in the first of the independent exhibitions mounted by Monet and his associates as an alternative to the Salon de Paris.Monet's ambition of documenting the French countryside led him to adopt a method of painting the same scene many times in order to capture the changing of light and the passing of the seasons. From 1883, Monet lived in Giverny, where he purchased a house and property and began a vast landscaping project which included lily ponds that would become the subjects of his best-known works. In 1899, he began painting the water lilies, first in vertical views with a Japanese bridge as a central feature and later in the series of large-scale paintings that was to occupy him continuously for the next 20 years of his life.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Monet         73
4   4       Rene Magritte  1898 - 1967      Surrealism,Impressionism     Belgian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                René François Ghislain Magritte (French: [ʁəne fʁɑ̃swa ɡilɛ̃ maɡʁit]; 21 November 1898 – 15 August 1967) was a Belgian Surrealist artist. He became well known for creating a number of witty and thought-provoking images. Often depicting ordinary objects in an unusual context, his work is known for challenging observers' preconditioned perceptions of reality. His imagery has influenced Pop art, minimalist and conceptual art.      http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/René_Magritte        194
```

questions.question: Are there any epochs or periods that feature an unusually high diversity of genres?

analysis.design_rationale: To directly answer whether some periods feature unusually high genre diversity, we need to aggregate artworks by their 'years' and count the number of unique 'genre' values per period. A bar chart allows clear comparison between epochs, highlighting any spikes or outliers in genre count. Since 'years' may represent a time span, we'll group artworks into broader periods (e.g., decade or century buckets) for meaningful aggregation. This approach is effective because 'genre' is categorical and 'years' is string, and a bar chart gives a clear, direct visual for diversity comparison across time.
analysis.target_insight: Identifies which time periods/epochs had the highest diversity of genres, revealing potential golden ages or unique bursts of creativity.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Bar width for visibility, sorted by genre diversity descending

transformations: Parse df['years'] to extract creation year and assign to epoch buckets (e.g., century)
For each epoch bucket, count number of unique genres (splitting multi-genre entries)
Sort epochs descending by genre diversity
data_preparation_steps: Extract year from df['years'] (using regex for the earliest year within each range)
Assign each artwork to a century (e.g., 1400s, 1500s...)
Split df['genre'] on delimiters (e.g., ',' or ';') and flatten genre list for each epoch
Aggregate unique genre count per century
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re

df = pd.read_csv('csv_c2_sec\20.csv')
# Extract creation year using regex (pick first 4-digit number)
def extract_year(s):
    years = re.findall(r'[0-9]{4}', str(s))
    return int(years[0]) if len(years) > 0 else np.nan

df['creation_year'] = df['years'].apply(extract_year)
def assign_century(year):
    if pd.isnull(year):
        return 'Unknown'
    century = (year // 100) * 100
    return f'{century}s'
df['century'] = df['creation_year'].apply(assign_century)
# Split genres and explode for unique counting
df['genre_list'] = df['genre'].str.split(',')
df_exploded = df.explode('genre_list')
df_exploded['genre_list'] = df_exploded['genre_list'].str.strip()
df_grouped = df_exploded.groupby('century')['genre_list'].nunique().reset_index()
df_grouped = df_grouped.rename(columns={'genre_list': 'unique_genre_count'})
df_grouped = df_grouped.sort_values('unique_genre_count', ascending=False)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,6))
ax.bar(df_grouped['century'], df_grouped['unique_genre_count'], color='skyblue')
ax.set_xlabel('Century')
ax.set_ylabel('Number of Unique Genres')
ax.set_title('Genre Diversity per Epoch (Century)\nBest Artworks of All Time')
for i, (century, cnt) in enumerate(zip(df_grouped['century'], df_grouped['unique_genre_count'])):
    ax.text(i, cnt + 0.2, str(cnt), ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap showing which genres appeared in which epochs reveals not only diversity but also the distribution and overlap of genres across time. It helps surface periods with unusually high genre variety—a period with multiple genres will show many colored cells in that row. This unconventional approach maps 'century' (rows) versus individual 'genre' (columns), with cell brightness representing genre presence.
analysis.target_insight: Visualizes both the spread and concentration of genres across epochs, identifying periods with notably broad genre representation and epochs with dominant genres.

chart_encodings.color: genre presence (binary: 1 if present, 0 if not)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Color intensity, readable tick labels, gridlines

transformations: Extract year from df['years'] and assign to century as in previous step
Expand df['genre'] to individual genre values
Build a binary matrix: rows=centuries, columns=genres, value=1 if genre present in century
data_preparation_steps: Extract creation_year and assign 'century' (same as previous suggestion)
Split df['genre'] into genre_list and explode rows for genre mapping
Pivot the data for a century x genre matrix, fill with 1 if genre present, 0 otherwise
chart_suggestions.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import re
import matplotlib as mpl

df = pd.read_csv('csv_c2_sec\20.csv')
def extract_year(s):
    years = re.findall(r'[0-9]{4}', str(s))
    return int(years[0]) if len(years) > 0 else np.nan

def assign_century(year):
    if pd.isnull(year):
        return 'Unknown'
    century = (year // 100) * 100
    return f'{century}s'
df['creation_year'] = df['years'].apply(extract_year)
df['century'] = df['creation_year'].apply(assign_century)
df['genre_list'] = df['genre'].str.split(',')
df_exploded = df.explode('genre_list')
df_exploded['genre_list'] = df_exploded['genre_list'].str.strip()
centuries_order = df_exploded['century'].drop_duplicates().sort_values()
genres_order = df_exploded['genre_list'].drop_duplicates().sort_values()
pivot = pd.pivot_table(df_exploded, values='id', index='century', columns='genre_list', aggfunc='count', fill_value=0)
pivot = pivot.applymap(lambda x: 1 if x > 0 else 0)
pivot = pivot.reindex(index=centuries_order, columns=genres_order)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14,8))
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis'].resampled(2)
m = ax.imshow(pivot.values, cmap=cmap, aspect='auto')
ax.set_xticks(np.arange(len(genres_order)))
ax.set_yticks(np.arange(len(centuries_order)))
ax.set_xticklabels(genres_order, rotation=90, fontsize=9)
ax.set_yticklabels(centuries_order, fontsize=10)
ax.set_xlabel('Genre')
ax.set_ylabel('Century')
ax.set_title('Heatmap of Genre Presence per Epoch\nBest Artworks of All Time')
plt.colorbar(m, ax=ax, label='Genre Presence')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

## Table 1: Best_Artworks_of_All_Time (50 rows × 8 columns)

### Description
Data loaded from CSV file: 20.csv (Dataset: Best Artworks of All Time)

### Schema (8 fields)

0   0   Amedeo Modigliani  1884 - 1920                 Expressionism     Italian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Amedeo Clemente Modigliani (Italian pronunciation: [ameˈdɛːo modiʎˈʎaːni]; 12 July 1884 – 24 January 1920) was an Italian Jewish painter and sculptor who worked mainly in France. He is known for portraits and nudes in a modern style characterized by elongation of faces, necks, and figures that were not received well during his lifetime but later found acceptance. Modigliani spent his youth in Italy, where he studied the art of antiquity and the Renaissance. In 1906 he moved to Paris, where he came into contact with such artists as Pablo Picasso and Constantin Brâncuși. By 1912 Modigliani was exhibiting highly stylized sculptures with Cubists of the Section d'Or group at the Salon d'Automne.  http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amedeo_Modigliani        193

2   2        Diego Rivera  1886 - 1957       Social Realism,Muralism     Mexican                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Diego María de la Concepción Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodríguez, known as Diego Rivera (Spanish pronunciation: [ˈdjeɣo riˈβeɾa]; December 8, 1886 – November 24, 1957) was a prominent Mexican painter. His large frescoes helped establish the Mexican mural movement in Mexican art. Between 1922 and 1953, Rivera painted murals in, among other places, Mexico City, Chapingo, Cuernavaca, San Francisco, Detroit, and New York City. In 1931, a retrospective exhibition of his works was held at the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Rivera had a volatile marriage with fellow Mexican artist Frida Kahlo.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diego_Rivera         70
3   3        Claude Monet  1840 - 1926                 Impressionism      French  Oscar-Claude Monet (; French: [klod mɔnɛ]; 14 November 1840 – 5 December 1926) was a French painter, a founder of French Impressionist painting and the most consistent and prolific practitioner of the movement's philosophy of expressing one's perceptions before nature, especially as applied to plein air landscape painting. The term "Impressionism" is derived from the title of his painting Impression, soleil levant (Impression, Sunrise), which was exhibited in 1874 in the first of the independent exhibitions mounted by Monet and his associates as an alternative to the Salon de Paris.Monet's ambition of documenting the French countryside led him to adopt a method of painting the same scene many times in order to capture the changing of light and the passing of the seasons. From 1883, Monet lived in Giverny, where he purchased a house and property and began a vast landscaping project which included lily ponds that would become the subjects of his best-known works. In 1899, he began painting the water lilies, first in vertical views with a Japanese bridge as a central feature and later in the series of large-scale paintings that was to occupy him continuously for the next 20 years of his life.       http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Monet         73
4   4       Rene Magritte  1898 - 1967      Surrealism,Impressionism     Belgian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                René François Ghislain Magritte (French: [ʁəne fʁɑ̃swa ɡilɛ̃ maɡʁit]; 21 November 1898 – 15 August 1967) was a Belgian Surrealist artist. He became well known for creating a number of witty and thought-provoking images. Often depicting ordinary objects in an unusual context, his work is known for challenging observers' preconditioned perceptions of reality. His imagery has influenced Pop art, minimalist and conceptual art.      http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/René_Magritte        194
